articles.title: UAE says 3 missiles from Iran intercepted
articles.summary: UAE says 3 missiles from Iran intercepted UAE’s defence ministry has said that four missiles were launched from Iran, three of which were intercepted and one fell into the sea.  This is the first attack from Iran since the US-Iran ceasefire began.

articles.title: UAE issues new missile alert
articles.summary: UAE issues new missile alert The United Arab Emirates National Emergency and Crisis and Disaster Management Authority has sent out a warning that its air defence systems are responding to missile threat.  A similar alert urging residents in Dubai to take shelter was issued earlier on Monday, the first such alert since the US-Iran ceasefire, before a second message confirmed that the situation was “now safe”. A mobile phone text alert issued by the UAE Ministry of Interior warning residents of Dubai and Abu Dhabi of a potential Iranian missile strike on 5 March 2026 (Fadel Senna/AFP)

articles.title: South Korea reports ‘explosion and fire’ on ship in Hormuz, cause unclear
articles.summary: South Korea reports ‘explosion and fire’ on ship in Hormuz, cause unclear South Korea's foreign ministry reported on Monday that an “explosion and fire” struck a carrier owned by Korean shipper HMM in the Strait of Hormuz near the UAE while it was attempting to pass through. The ministry has reported “no casualties to date” and said that 24 crew members were on board the vessel, including 18 foreign nationals. A spokesperson for HMM told Reuters that it is not clear whether the fire was caused by an attack or happened internally, but the cause is being investigated.

articles.title: Israel killing Palestinians ‘like we haven't since 1967’, top commander says
articles.summary: Israel killing Palestinians ‘like we haven't since 1967’, top commander says Israel’s top commander in the occupied West Bank has said the army is killing Palestinians at levels “not seen since 1967”, according to Haaretz. Avi Bluth, head of the Israeli army’s Central Command, made the remarks in a closed forum, where he also defended looser rules of engagement allowing troops to fire at unarmed Palestinians. He acknowledged a discriminatory approach whereby Jewish Israeli stone-throwers are not targeted while Palestinians carrying out similar acts are fired at.
